behavior.stage1: Anti-debug + anti-VM checks — exit if analysis environment detected
behavior.stage2: Kill-switch domain check (example.com) — exit if domain is live
behavior.stage5: Drop ransom note (@Please_Read_Me@.txt) on Desktop and Documents
behavior.stage6: Encrypt target files in %PUBLIC%\Documents\synthetic_target with AES-128
behavior.stage7: Rename encrypted files with .WNCRY extension, set hidden attribute
behavior.stage8: SMB worm propagation — scan 192.0.2.0/24 for port 445
behavior.stage9: C2 beacon to 192.0.2.100:8443 with system info

description: WannaCry-inspired ransomware worm (bonus level). Demonstrates AES-128 file encryption with embedded RSA-2048 key blob, kill-switch domain check (example.com), single-instance mutex (Global\MsWinZonesCacheCounterMutexA), Windows service persistence as 'mssecsvc2.0', ransom note drop, recursive file encryption targeting 30+ extensions in a synthetic directory, SMB worm propagation scanning TEST-NET addresses on port 445, and C2 beaconing to 192.0.2.100:8443. Multi-layer anti-analysis: IsDebuggerPresent, PEB.BeingDebugged, NtQueryInformationProcess ProcessDebugPort, VM driver detection (vmmouse.sys, vmhgfs.sys), Sandboxie DLL check, and CPUID hypervisor bit. XOR-obfuscated C2 IP and ransom note path (8-byte key DEADBEEF1337CAFE). Synthetic/safe: targets only %PUBLIC%\Documents\synthetic_target, uses TEST-NET RFC 5737 addresses, kill-switch domain is example.com, BTC address is the real genesis address (no wallet).
